cards.back: A logical clock is a theoretical clock that is used to represent the order of events in a distributed system, without depending on the real world clock.

cards.front: What is the main difference between a logical clock and a real clock?
cards.back: A logical clock is not synchronized with a real clock, and its time value is only relative to the events it is observing.

cards.front: What is the purpose of a logical clock in distributed systems?
cards.back: The purpose of a logical clock is to maintain a consistent view of time across all nodes in the system, allowing for the coordination of events and processes.
A broadcast clock is a type of logical clock that broadcasts its time value to all nodes in the system, allowing them to synchronize their clocks.

cards.front: What is the difference between a synchronous and asynchronous logical clock?
cards.back: A synchronous logical clock is synchronized with a real clock, while an asynchronous logical clock is not synchronized with a real clock and relies on the broadcast of time values to coordinate events.
